label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options did NOT contribute to Nikki's reunion with her family. The options are (A) her father, (B) 192.com, (C) her son, and (D) her mother-in-law. First, I need to go through the text again to make sure I remember all the details. Nikki was raised by her father from a very early age, and her sister by their mother. They had contact when Nikki was 6 but then it stopped. Now, with a husband and son, she decides to find her family. She didn't know where to start but her husband's mum (mother-in-law) recommended 192.com. With her son's help, she searched for her sister's name on the electoral roll. The site gave two results in their old area. She called the first number and it was her sister. Then her sister gave the number to their mom, who called Nikki. They arranged to meet and reunite. Nikki mentions that 192.com made it easy, and they weren't on social media, so that wouldn't have worked. So the contributors are her son (helped with the search), mother-in-law (recommended 192.com), and 192.com (the service that found the contact). The father is mentioned as raising Nikki, but he's not part of the recent reunion. The father's role was in her upbringing, but the reunion happened through her sister and mother contacting via the search, so her father didn't
